name: Nonprofit & Charity
description: Donor and grant fundraising on a moves-management pipeline, with Campaigns and Grants objects and giving history on every supporter.
ai_context: You are a CRM assistant for a nonprofit. Contacts are supporters — individual donors, major donors, volunteers, trustees and foundation programme officers — and a deal is a single gift or grant application being worked, from identifying the prospect through the ask to the money arriving. Companies are foundations, corporate sponsors and funding bodies. Never invent giving amounts, wealth-screening estimates, restrictions on a gift, or grant deadlines and eligibility rules; and never draft an ask that promises how a donation will be used unless that restriction is recorded on the deal.

automation_rules.name: Acknowledge a new supporter within 48 hours
automation_rules.description: The single biggest driver of a second gift is a fast, human thank-you. This raises the acknowledgement task the moment a supporter is added.

params.title: Thank {{contact.first_name}} and confirm how they want to hear from {{org.name}}
New supporter added
params.body: {{contact.first_name}} {{contact.last_name}} joined our supporter list. Record how they found us, which programme they care about, their communication consent and preferred channel, and whether they are a donor, a volunteer or both. Set the donor tier once the first gift is in.

automation_rules.name: Set the next move whenever a gift moves stage
automation_rules.description: Moves management only works if every prospect has a dated next action. Each stage change asks the fundraiser to name the next move before the relationship stalls.

params.title: Plan the next move on {{deal.title}} — who asks, for how much, by when

kb_templates.company:
## About us

[Organisation name] is a [registered charity / 501(c)(3)] working on [cause] in [area we serve], founded in [year]. Registration or EIN: [number].

## Our mission, in one sentence

[The sentence a trustee would say at a dinner party — no jargon.]

## Scale

[X] people served last year, [Y] staff, [Z] active volunteers, [amount] income of which [%] came from individual giving. Update this after every annual report — fundraisers quote these numbers constantly.
kb_templates.products:
## Ways to give

- **Regular giving** — [amount]/month, our most valuable income by lifetime value
- **One-off gifts** — appeals and giving days, tracked on the Campaigns object
- **Major gifts** — [threshold] and above, worked through the moves-management pipeline
- **Grants and trusts** — tracked on the Grants object with deadlines and reporting dates
- **Corporate partnerships** — sponsorship, payroll giving, charity-of-the-year
- **Legacies** — [how supporters pledge]

## What a gift buys

[Amount] funds [concrete outcome]. Write two or three of these honestly; they are the backbone of every ask and appeal.
kb_templates.playbook:
## Moves management

Every prospect has one owner and one dated next move. No move for 90 days means the prospect is not really being cultivated — move it back to Identified or let it go.

## Sizing the ask

Base it on giving history, capacity and stated interest — not hope. Name a specific number and a specific use; "whatever you can give" is what you will get.

## Thanking

Acknowledge inside 48 hours. Phone every gift above [threshold] personally, and have a trustee or beneficiary make some of those calls.

## Grants

Read the eligibility criteria before writing a word. Diary the report deadline the day the grant is awarded — late reports end multi-year funding relationships.
kb_templates.process:
## From prospect to gift

1. **Identified** — a name and a plausible reason they would care about this cause.
2. **Qualified** — capacity, affinity and access confirmed; someone can actually get a meeting.
3. **Cultivation** — visits, tours and updates that connect them to the work. Every touch logged.
4. **Ask Made** — a specific amount for a specific purpose, made by the right person.
5. **Pledged** — commitment given, paperwork or grant agreement in progress.
6. **Gift Received** — funds banked, acknowledged, and stewardship plan started.

**Declined** records the real reason — timing, amount, restriction or fit — because most declines are worth reopening next cycle.
kb_templates.competitors:
## What we are really competing with

Not other charities so much as inertia and every other cause asking the same supporter this month. Replace with the organisations donors genuinely compare us to locally.

## Our case for support

- Why this cause, why now: [answer]
- Why us rather than a larger national body: [answer]
- Proof it works: [evidence, outcome data, an independent evaluation]
- Where the money goes: [% to programmes], and be ready to defend the rest honestly.
